item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty interesting. It reminds me of Godzilla and Evangelion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation isn't necessarily monitoring documents that contain this simple word. For example, if a memetic phrase existed and the Foundation was looking for it, they wouldn't look for every little thing that has one of the words of the said phrase in it. However, now that I look back on it, I could have made it much clearer. I'll get onto editing that.

The thing is, SCP-3534's anomalous properties only manifest when inscribed in a submarine, not a piece of paper. So the Foundation is actually just keeping a lookout for 4/5 of the characters or all of the characters next to one another in a document; even then, nothing bad can happen until those characters make it into a submarine's walls. Again, I'll make this more clear in the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As documentation containing SCP-3534 is still being manufactured within Japan, personnel … to investigate unauthorized SCP-3534 documentation manufacturing. All individuals found documenting elements of SCP-3534 are to be taken into Foundation custody for interrogation.

Do you mean this paragraph? ..then, documentation containing "永", one of the five SCP-3534 instances, is also being manufactured in China and any other regions or countries that contains Chinese-speaking people. "永" is just the Chinese word for "forever" or "eternity". I don't know how the charecter is used in Japan, but in China, whenever people want so say "forever", or "permanent" or something like that, one of SCP-3534 manifests.
If I understand this correctly.. I wonder if containing SCP-3534 in this way is even.. feasible? MTF cannot investigate every books and documents that mentioned "forever", right? The Foundation cannot interrogate every people that hold pieces of paper saying "forever", right? Another interesting fact about "永" is that, it also serves as a "standard model" on copybooks for calligraphy. Something like "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog."

Given the containment procedures and description, I wonder if the author is aware of that fact..

Edit: Checked the Wikitionary. "永" is still in use in Chinese, Japanese, Korean and Vietnamese language.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't that the point? It even says the characters occur in contemporary documents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image 1, ”永” is a quite common Chinese character. It is written in 楷体, a font that is widely used in China. Don’t know if this matters, but seeing one of the five anomalous thing is something we use everyday just.. break the immersion at the very beginning.

I guess the author may try combine several characters together to create a new character? So that it can look strange enough..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll go ahead and reread the addendum and see if I can simply rephrase some things to make them flow off the tongue easier.

Thank you for your feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the idea was interesting but the quality of the writing was quite inconsistent. I particularly had issues with with that last addendum where I noticed some grammar issues and odd word choices (for example you use ‘absolutely’ three times in the first paragraph).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot as part of war on all fronts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting that the doomsday isn't occurring in the baseline.

item.title: Re: Homeless Heterotrophs In-depth review
item.content:encoded:
I have undertaken the laborious transfer on your behalf.
– hide block

Homeless heterotroph review. SCP-3534

Verdict: Downvote.

Reasoning

  • While the article is well written, it’s simply not an article yet. SCP-3534 is written in a way that, as i understand from reading it over 3 times, dodges around having to explain to the reader what SCP-3534-3 even is.
  • The audacious and gung-ho containment procedures seem to scream to me that i should expect an article entailing an audacious and gung-ho skip. But i don’t get one because i don’t even get a glimpse of what the danger is. Extra-dimensional Fighters and blowing-up sites in retaliation to a single event is cool and all that, but i’m given no context as a reader to believe that it's a necessary precaution for the containment of a skip that has no description.
  • In my opinion, and i am adamant that this opinion is correct, SCP-3534-1 itself is simply a quick alternative to addressing the actual “Subject” of the skip itself. SCP-3534 seems to me to be false-front for the real “meat” of the article. If this where rewritten and and SCP-3534-1 and SCP-3534-3 where switched, then it would be much more relevant to the containment procedures and the article itself.
  • Despite the fact that this “Sekrit boat grafite code” is the harbinger of end-times. The article tells the reader 20% of the DIU submarine-of-doom’s activation code with the image. Aesthetic appeal is far less important than pragmatic article structure.
  • Possibly the most concerning part of this articles composition to me is how Site-self-absorbed the article is in general. This is not a user-friendly article in the slightest. a reader who doesn’t know an of the sites many side-canons has absolutely no boramiter of what is going on in this article.
    • “SCP-3534-2, Article I, Section 1 of the Hy-Brasil International Security Agreement of 1992 (Footnote) A binding treaty enacted by King Delbáeth II of Hy-Brasil between the Foundation, the Global Occult Coalition and several governments concerning the reappearance of entities similar to UAE-Brasil-78.
      • I appreciate how cleverly you blended this into the article. But this is asking way to much of the reader. We aren't all readerbots, and not all readerbots read the tome of “Site tales”

Written in 47 minutes by Homeless Heterotroph, Friday may 18th 2018

Somewhat relevant link for comedy
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pNFF1ZTl1oY

item.title: Re: Homeless Heterotrophs In-depth review
item.content:encoded: Vote change: In light of your recent edits, i've changed my vote to a no-vote.

item.title: Re: Homeless Heterotrophs In-depth review
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the feedback! It will be read over and taken into consideration.

item.title: Homeless Heterotrophs In-depth review
item.content:encoded:
Written in a google-doc because i'm much better with that then in the Writing Box of 9.5pointfontVerdina. If there is any problems with this kind of review let me know and i will happily laboriously transfer it to said 9.5pointfontbox.

I down-voted this skip, this is why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went ahead and removed unnecessary crosslinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like this. Unfortunately, there are just way too many moving parts for me to enjoy. There's also far too many crosslinks here, which kind of negatively impacts my enjoyment of this skip.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved what's going on in the article but I think the log at the end was a bit off in trying to flesh out the scenario more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oddly enough, turns out Hydrocephalus is also a genus of Trilobite, so you could call that a happy accident.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the feedback! I didn't even notice that I said hydrocephalous.

I'll revise the wording of certain phrases sometime soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is interesting and I did mostly enjoy it, but you've got a lot of weird word and grammar choices throughout the article that make things difficult to understand. I wasn't keeping a list unfortunately but, for example:

Instances exhibit characteristics of both reptiles and aquatic hydrocephalus,

Even after googling this I have no idea what you are trying to describe. As far as I can tell, hydrocephalus is some kind of condition to do with fluid in the brain.

item.title: Doomsyesterday
item.content:encoded:
Doomsday already happened. Just.. not here.

My entry for the Doomsday Contest. Thank you to my fellow teammates in Team Mikasa for helping revise: stormbreath, DrChandra, Stallmantic!

More importantly, thank you for reading.

Image sources:
Image 1 - Japanese symbol for eternity. Still of the animation towards the middle of the page.
Image 2 - Photo of Type-A Kō-hyōteki-class submarine from Wikipedia.
